title: Electric Propulsion Manufacturing
entry_mechanism: Electric propulsion company establishing Saudi manufacturing under PSAP Track 1
executive_summary:
Electric propulsion — ion thrusters, Hall-effect thrusters, and cold-gas propulsion systems for satellite attitude control and orbit maintenance — has become the standard propulsion technology for small and medium satellites. Unlike chemical propulsion (which requires explosive propellants and carries significant regulatory complexity), electric propulsion systems use inert propellants (xenon, iodine) and are far more accessible for manufacturing localisation.

Saudi Arabia's aspiration for a sovereign satellite constellation requires domestic propulsion capability — importing thrusters from foreign manufacturers creates a critical operational dependency that can be disrupted at the worst moments. Electric propulsion manufacturing (thruster assembly, power processing unit electronics, propellant management hardware) uses manufacturing processes accessible to Saudi Arabia's electronics and precision machining base.

The global electric propulsion market was valued at $1.2 billion in 2024 growing at 16.8% CAGR. Saudi Arabia's satellite constellation demand creates initial domestic demand, with GCC satellite operator export potential by Phase III.

companies.name: Safran Spacecraft Propulsion
companies.rationale: Safran is Europe's leading spacecraft propulsion manufacturer. Non-ITAR French technology with Saudi Arabia government relationships.

companies.name: Busek
companies.rationale: Busek manufactures Hall-effect thrusters for small satellites. Some ITAR considerations but accessible technologies.

companies.name: Bradford ECAPS (Sweden)
companies.rationale: Bradford ECAPS develops green monopropellant systems — safer and more accessible than conventional propellants. Non-ITAR Swedish technology.

companies.name: Enpulsion (Austria)
companies.rationale: Enpulsion manufactures compact ion propulsion systems for small satellites. Non-ITAR, accessible international partner.

companies.name: Exotrail (France)
companies.rationale: Exotrail is a French startup producing compact Hall-effect thrusters. Non-ITAR, active international partnerships.

partners.name: SSA / Riyadh Smart Parcel
partners.upgrade: Safran or Enpulsion JV; thruster assembly and test facility; xenon propellant handling; 15 Saudi propulsion engineers; first Saudi-assembled thruster on Saudi satellite

partners.name: KACST Propulsion Research
partners.current: Some plasma physics research; combustion research
partners.upgrade: GADD-KACST electric propulsion research programme; Hall-effect thruster plasma simulation; thruster performance characterisation facility

partners.name: KFUPM Mechanical/Aerospace Engineering
partners.current: Fluid mechanics; propulsion theory; plasma physics
partners.upgrade: Space propulsion engineering specialisation; Safran-KFUPM research partnership; graduate placement at Saudi propulsion facility

partners.name: Saudi Aramco Research Centre
partners.current: Materials and chemical engineering; some plasma research
partners.upgrade: Propellant materials research; xenon handling and storage expertise from industrial gas experience; dual-use propulsion materials research
